title: Can't seem to get rid of red exclamation in formula

posts.author: jbrown (John Brown)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve looked at a lot of posts about this, and I just don’t fully understand the code being used to remove the red exclamation when my formula has to divide by zero when the fields are blank.
The formula :
ROUNDUP({Reimbursment}/{Target}*100,1)

Its just finding a percentage, and when I don’t have the numbers to put in yet the exclamation point is there. It doesn’t hurt anything it just looks bad, and I wish I could just hide it somehow. Thanks.

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi John,

Try:

ROUNDUP({Reimbursment}/IF({Target}>0,{Target},1)*100,1)

It will show 0 when no data in {Target} or {Reimbursment}.

Looking for a simpler way to write complex formulas? Check out the Advanced Formula Booster at https://mdboosters.com. It’s a convenient third-party app that simplifies formula writing. With it, you can spread your formulas across several lines, utilize variables, and access dozens of functions not found in the standard formula column.

posts.author: jbrown (John Brown)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you Gilles. It does get rid of the exclamation, but at the bottom I have it showing the average of all the percentages in this column, so it lowers the average a lot to have all the extra 0’s. Is there a way for it to not even show anything before I’ve filled in the reimbursement and target numbers? Thanks again for the help.

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
jbrown (John Brown)
posts.cookedHtml:
It worked! Thank you Gilles
I would have never figured that out haha
